synapse_dwh.description: eToro Synapse Data Warehouse — DWH layer with ETL transformations from production sources

supplementary_knowledge_schemas.description: Schemas that are NOT documentation targets but contain essential knowledge for lineage, ETL source discovery, and column descriptions. Their DDLs in the SSDT repo should be read during Phases 2, 4, 5, 8, 9, and 10A/10B. Constitution II: blacklisted objects are lineage inputs.

schemas.name: DWH_Migration
schemas.purpose: Legacy on-prem DWH SQL Server migration staging. Tables here were migrated to DWH_dbo in Sept 2024. Contains frozen dimension definitions (e.g., Dim_AccountType, Dim_Position) unique to Synapse. No wiki needed, but DDLs should be read for column structure AND live tables should be sampled for value maps in Phase 2 B1b.
schemas.input_use: Lookup/dimension resolution (Phase 4), JOIN target recognition (Phase 5), lineage source attribution (Phase 10A/10B). Query live Synapse table for value maps.

schemas.name: BI_DB_Migration
schemas.purpose: Legacy on-prem BI_DB production migration staging. Tables migrated from the BI_DB production SQL Server.

schemas.name: DWH_staging
schemas.purpose: Active lake-to-Synapse pipeline staging tables. 135 tables staging data from the data lake before ETL SPs load them into DWH_dbo/BI_DB_dbo. Column names reveal production source table naming.
schemas.input_use: Column rename discovery (Phase 9), ETL source identification (Phase 2 B1).

schemas.name: CopyFromLake
schemas.purpose: Alternative lake import tables. Used by some schemas for direct lake-to-table loads instead of external tables.
schemas.input_use: Circular import detection — equivalent to external tables. Trace through to production source (Phase 2 B2, Phase 10A Step 1c).

external_table_paths.description: External table SSDT repo paths for ALL documentation target schemas. AUTHORITATIVE LIST — Phase 2 B2 and Phase 10A Step 1c MUST read this config for the schema list instead of hardcoding schemas. When a new schema is added to the 'schemas' array above, add its External Tables path here too. External tables are NOT documentation targets but are essential lineage inputs.

opsdb_orchestration.description: Static snapshots of OpsDB ETL orchestration metadata. Use these INSTEAD of querying OpsDB MCP. Covers BI_DB_dbo, DWH_dbo, Dealing_dbo schemas. Refresh manually via OpsDB MCP only if objects are missing.
opsdb_orchestration.objects_status_note: 710 active objects: ProcedureName, TableName, Priority (0=first, 99=last), FrequencySP, ProcessType (1=SQL, 4=FinanceReportSPS), ProcessName
opsdb_orchestration.procedure_dependencies_note: 1149 rows: Main rows (SP→Table) + Dependency rows (SP reads from other SP outputs). RowType='Main row' or 'Dependencie row'

priority_semantics.0: Base layer — no intra-schema dependencies, reads from DWH/production directly
priority_semantics.15: Second wave — depends on Priority 0 outputs (e.g., CMR Phase2)
priority_semantics.20: Third wave — depends on Priority 0 and 15 outputs
priority_semantics.60: DDR fact tables — heavy cross-object aggregation
priority_semantics.99: FinanceReportSPS — run LAST, depend on everything else

generic_pipeline_mapping.static_fallback_note: View has column-name misalignment from position 11 onward. Static file uses corrected position-based extraction. Refresh with _parse_mapping.py when needed.
generic_pipeline_mapping.description: Unity Catalog view that maps production source tables to their lake/DWH targets via the Generic Pipeline. Use this to trace lineage from any Synapse table back to its production source. Falls back to static JSON when Databricks MCP is unavailable.

object_blacklist.description: Objects matching these patterns are SKIPPED during documentation — frozen/archived tables not worth documenting. Applied across ALL target schemas.

name_patterns.reason: HOLD suffix = decommissioned/archived table. Pipeline stopped, data frozen. Not worth documenting or deploying to UC.

explicit_blacklist.reason: DDR aggregation pyramid — too wide (172 cols), deferred
DDR auxiliary metrics sidecar for CID_Level, deferred
DDR aggregation pyramid — too wide (137 cols), deferred
DDR auxiliary metrics sidecar for Daily_Aggregated, deferred
DDR internal process monitoring, not an analytics table
DDR aggregation pyramid — too wide (141 cols), deferred

ssdt_repo.description: Dataplatform SSDT project — the version-controlled source of truth for ALL Synapse SQL Pool DDLs, SP code, view definitions, and function definitions. Constitution IX: ALWAYS read from here, NEVER query Synapse MCP for structural metadata.

function_foundations.description: For BI_DB_dbo and downstream schemas, functions contain foundational business logic (revenue calculations, population segmentation, trading volumes, DDR aggregations, PnL, AUM, MIMO). Phase 9 MUST read called functions from the SSDT repo and trace their logic into column descriptions. Functions are NOT documentation targets but are critical inputs — their computations define column semantics in the tables that use them.
function_foundations.phase_9_instruction: When tracing column assignments in a writer SP, if the SP calls a function (e.g., dbo.Function_Trading_Volume), READ the function definition from the SSDT repo. Extract the calculation logic and embed it concisely in the column description. The function IS the business logic — without reading it, the column description is meaningless.

upstream_knowledge_sources.name: production_trading_platform
upstream_knowledge_sources.description: Semantic wiki for the eToro trading platform production SQL Server — covers Trade, Customer, and Dictionary schemas with full column descriptions, FK resolution, business logic, and procedure analysis. Generated by the sql-semantic-doc pipeline in the DB_Schema repository.

upstream_knowledge_sources.name: dwh_dbo_wiki
upstream_knowledge_sources.description: Semantic wiki for the DWH_dbo schema in Synapse — BI_DB tables read heavily from DWH_dbo dimensions and facts. Column descriptions from DWH_dbo docs are Tier 1 sources for BI_DB documentation.

confluence_sources.description: Data Platform documentation — DWH schemas, ETL processes, Synapse objects

blacklist.canonical_description: ETL metadata: timestamp when this row was last updated by the ETL pipeline. EXCEPTION: for frozen migration tables (DWH_Migration schema origin), this is the original production timestamp preserved from the legacy system — NOT set by GETDATE(). Run timestamp analysis (Phase 2 Tier A1) to determine which applies before using this description.

ETL metadata: timestamp when this row was first inserted by the ETL pipeline. EXCEPTION: for frozen migration tables (DWH_Migration schema origin), this is the original creation timestamp preserved from the legacy system — NOT set by GETDATE(). Run timestamp analysis (Phase 2 Tier A1) to determine which applies before using this description.
ETL metadata: timestamp when this row was first created by the ETL pipeline
ETL metadata: timestamp when this row was first created
ETL metadata: source file name from lake ingestion
Fivetran metadata: timestamp when this row was last synced by Fivetran
ETL infrastructure: internal row identifier used by the ingestion pipeline
Data quality infrastructure: flag indicating whether this row passed DROP expectations checks
